title: Build LLM Apps with LangChain
modules: LangChain Architecture: Chains, Prompts, and the Execution Model
Connecting to Models: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Open-Source Alternatives
Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Embeddings, Vector Stores, and Document Loaders
Memory and Conversation State: Making Apps Context-Aware
Tool Use and Function Calling Inside Chains
Structuring Output: Parsers, Validators, and Typed Responses
Apply It: Build a Domain-Specific Assistant for Your Own Use Case
Deployment, Observability, and LangSmith for Production Debugging
synopsis: LangChain is the dominant framework for connecting LLMs to real-world data, tools, and workflows — and understanding its primitives is now a baseline skill for any professional building AI-powered software. This course moves fast through core concepts and lands on a working, deployable application you design around your own domain.
rationale: LangChain has become the de facto orchestration layer for LLM applications, yet most professionals lack structured knowledge of its architecture, making them dependent on fragile tutorial code they cannot adapt or debug.
learning_outcome: Professionals can design, build, and deploy a functional LangChain-powered application that connects an LLM to external data sources and tools relevant to their own work context.
source_signals: Building a Virtual Machine Inside ChatGPT (score: 4803)
Open source AI is the path forward (score: 5021)
nearest_existing: Magic you understand is a tool.
